title: ambulia gratioloides
intro: Ambulia gratioloides, also known as water hyssop or Indian water hyssop, is a perennial aquatic plant native to tropical Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Ambulia gratioloides include treating fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues, as well as being used as a digestive aid and to reduce inflammation. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and poultices, which are applied topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
